name: Bottom-Up DP
summary: The ways to reach step i equal the ways to reach step i-1 plus the ways to reach step i-2 — fill a table from the bottom up.
visualizationIntent: init: draw the dp table as a row of cells, all zero, with dp[0] and dp[1] seeded to 1. loop: a blue pointer i walks left to right across the table. update: the current cell dp[i] lights up while its two source cells dp[i-1] and dp[i-2] flash blue, and the readout shows the instantiated recurrence dp[i] = dp[i-1] + dp[i-2]; already-filled cells stay pale. return: the answer cell dp[n] turns green and its value is the number of distinct ways.

lineExplanations.3: Create a table with one slot for every step from 0 up to n, all starting at zero.
lineExplanations.4: Base case: there is exactly one way to stand at the bottom (do nothing).
lineExplanations.5: Base case: there is exactly one way to reach the first step (a single 1-step).
lineExplanations.6: Fill the table from step 2 up to step n, one step at a time.
lineExplanations.7: Ways to reach step i = ways to reach step i-1 (take a 1-step) plus ways to reach step i-2 (take a 2-step).
lineExplanations.8: The answer is the number of ways stored in the last cell, dp[n].
lineExplanations.9: Return the total number of distinct ways to reach the top.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: defines the class wrapper LeetCode expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def climbStairs(self, n): defines the method; n is the number of stairs.
syntaxExplanations.3: [0] * (n + 1) builds a list of n+1 zeros.
syntaxExplanations.4: dp[0] = 1 stores 1 in the first slot of the list.
syntaxExplanations.5: dp[1] = 1 stores 1 in the second slot of the list.
syntaxExplanations.6: for i in range(2, n + 1): loops i over 2, 3, ..., n.
syntaxExplanations.7: dp[i] = dp[i - 1] + dp[i - 2] adds the two previous slots into slot i.
syntaxExplanations.8: result = dp[n] reads the last slot into a variable.
syntaxExplanations.9: return result hands the answer back to the caller.
